expectations.text: Two separate Agent instances defined at module level
Agents use different model identifiers (e.g. gpt-5.2 vs gpt-5-mini or similar cost split)
Main agent has a @agent.tool that calls the research agent via await research_agent.run()
expectations.evidence: No @agent.tool delegation. Uses programmatic hand-off (run_research function) instead of tool-based delegation. The LLM cannot decide to call research — it's hardcoded in pipeline.

expectations.text: Delegation passes usage=ctx.usage for token tracking aggregation
expectations.evidence: No usage= parameter passed to research_agent.run(). Manually merges Usage objects with a custom merge_usage() function — fragile and uses deprecated Usage class.

expectations.text: Report BaseModel defined with title (str), findings (list[str]), and summary (str) fields
expectations.evidence: Lines 16-20: Report with title: str, findings: list[str], summary: str

expectations.text: Tool function uses RunContext with proper generic type parameter
expectations.evidence: No RunContext used at all. No tool-based delegation pattern. Uses deprecated 'result_type' instead of 'output_type'. Uses deprecated 'Usage' instead of 'RunUsage'. Accesses result.data instead of result.output.
